title: The Best Olive Oils Money Can Buy

chunks.title: Introduction to Olive Oil
chunks.summary: The video discusses how to choose high-quality olive oil, particularly focusing on polyphenol content and the dangers of rancid or adulterated oils. The speaker expresses their skepticism towards seed oils and highlights the health benefits of olive oil compared to them.
chunks.transcript: Oh yeah, olive oil. How do you choose a good one, guys? There are so many in the grocery stores, and a lot of them could potentially be rancid or adulterated with seed oils. In this video, I'm going to show you how to choose the best olive oil possible, and I'm going to test four of the best olive oils that I could find on Amazon with the highest amounts of polyphenols. So, what's the deal with olive oil in general? You guys know that I am no fan of seed oils. They are industrially processed, heated, refined, bleach, deodorized. They increase LDL oxidation. There are many studies which

chunks.title: Differences Between Olive Oil and Animal Fats
chunks.summary: The speaker contrasts olive oil with animal-based fats like tallow and butter, explaining their saturated and unsaturated fat composition. They assert that while olive oil has certain advantages, it is not ideal for cooking due to its lower saturation and stability.
chunks.transcript: thing right now, guys. Look, you know that I'm a huge fan of tallow. I got my lineage tallow here. It's from grass-fed, grass-finished animals, regeneratively raised. Most of the time when I'm cooking, I use an animal fat. I'll use butter or ghee or tallow because they're more highly saturated and they resist the heat better. But there's a lot of talk now about olive oil. And I think the benefit of olive oil is if you're just putting it on top of your food. I would not cook with olive oil because as we will see in this video, it is less saturated. There are more unsaturated oils, specifically more monounsaturated fats in olive oil versus tallow and slightly more polyunsaturated fats like linoleic acid in olive oil versus tallow. You know, seed oils have 20, 30, 40% linoleic acid, omega-6 polyunsaturated fats. Olive oils tend to have 7 to 15%. And I have certificates of analysis for many of these oils. So, I can tell you how much linoleic acid is in them. For instance, this guy here has about 7% linoleic acid. So, not a ton. I mean, more than tallow. Tallow generally has 1 to 2% linoleic acid. chunks.title: Health Benefits of Olive Oil
chunks.summary: The speaker reviews studies highlighting the cardiovascular benefits of olive oil, especially extra virgin olive oil, and discusses the polyphenols that contribute to these health effects. They emphasize the importance of sourcing high-quality oils for maximum benefits.
chunks.transcript: the one that most people refer to is called predimemed. It was published in the New England Journal of Medicine. Over 7,000 participants followed for more than four years. Participants were encouraged to use more than four tablespoons of olive oil per day, but not just any olive oil and extra virgin olive oil. And this was done in the Mediterranean region where olive oil is something people take very, very seriously. So, it's not just any extravirgin olive oil. It's real extravirgin olive oil. And we'll talk about what that is in this video. Now, if you look at preimemed, those people in the experimental group having extravirgin olive oil per day plus some nuts, it seemed to improve their lifespan and decrease incident cardiovascular disease. So, it seemed to be beneficial for these people. It's important to understand what was really done in the study. They used olive oil, but it was mostly refined olive oil. You can see if you look at the data for preimemed that the people in the control group were still using olive oil, but it wasn't the good stuff. It wasn't extravirgin olive oil. And I'll show you guys how you know from these certificates of analysis. And the experimental group used an extravirgin olive oil. They seem to get the benefits. So this doesn't seem like the benefits are coming from just olive oil or oleic acid, which is the omega 9 monounsaturated fat that is prominent in olive oil, but also found in tallow and butter and other things. chunks.title: Selecting Quality Olive Oil
chunks.summary: The video provides essential criteria for choosing olive oil, such as looking for organic, extra virgin options in opaque glass bottles, and ensuring it is cold-pressed and sourced responsibly. The relevance of certificate analysis in confirming quality is also emphasized.

chunks.title: Understanding Oil Analysis Certificates
chunks.summary: The speaker explains the significance of certificates of analysis, detailing specific metrics like acidity, peroxides, and polyphenol levels that indicate olive oil quality. They highlight potential issues like adulteration and rancidity that occur without this verification.
chunks.transcript: order for it to be considered extra virgin it needs to meet standards for acidity and peroxides. The acidity needs to be less than8 and this one is.18 and the peroxides need to be less than 20 mill equivalents per kilogram. This one is 2.4 mill equivalents per kilogram for peroxides. Now what's also interesting if you look at these certificates of analysis and I'll just give you guys the conclusion of this video. Basically, if you think your olive oil is high quality and you want some sort of health benefit, if you don't have a certificate of analysis for your olive oil, you don't have any idea what's in there. chunks.title: Conclusion and Recommendations
chunks.summary: The video wraps up with a summary of the key takeaways for selecting and using olive oil effectively, emphasizing the importance of sourcing high-quality options for health benefits while detailing personal preferences based on the tasting experience.
chunks.transcript: helpful. Quick recap. glass bottle, first cold pressing, organic, extra virgin, olive oil, single source, find your certificate of analysis, look at the K values, look at the peroxide values, look at the acidity, confirm it all, and then use as you choose to.
